input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule T.4.2.10 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.4.2.10
            TITLE: when an analogue signal is used, the apps will be considered to have failed when they

            CONTENT:
            rule T.4.2.10: When an analogue signal is used, the APPS will be considered to have failed when they
T.4.2.10 When an analogue signal is used, the APPS will be considered to have failed when they 
achieve an open circuit or short circuit condition which generates a signal outside of the 
normal operating range, for example <0.5 V or >4.5 V.   
The circuitry used to evaluate the sensor must use pull down or pull up resistors to ensure 
that open circuit signals result in a failure being detected.

final_answer: When an analogue signal is used, the APPS will be considered to have failed when they achieve an open circuit or short circuit condition which generates a signal outside of the normal operating range, for example <0.5 V or >4.5 V. The circuitry used to evaluate the sensor must use pull down or pull up resistors to ensure that open circuit signals result in a failure being detected.
